revision.text:
{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Omishinkanji kofun | native_name =小見真観寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Omishinkanji Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|34|N|139|28|36|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[schist]] quarried in the [[Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/omisinkanjikofun.html Gyoda City Board of Education] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gyōda]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[schist]] quarried in the [[Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high

The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[schist]] quarried in the [[Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high

A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Omishinkanji kofun | native_name =小見真観寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Omishinkanji Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|34|N|139|28|36|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[schist]] quarried in the [[Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[schist]] quarried in the [[Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Omishinkanji kofun | native_name =小見真観寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Omishinkanji Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|34|N|139|28|36|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[schist]] quarried in the [[Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Omishinkanji kofun | native_name =小見真観寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Omishinkanji Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|34|N|139|28|36|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[schist]] quarried in the [[Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Omishinkanji kofun | native_name =小見真観寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Omishinkanji Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|09|34|N|139|28|36|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[schist]] quarried in the [[Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[Chichibu Railway]] [[Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[:en:Schist|Schist]] quarried in the [[:en:Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Bushū-Araki Station|Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omi Shinkanji Kofun]][[en:Omishinkanji Kofun]][[ja:小見真観寺古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11463662]] Wikidata name: Omishinkanji Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Omishinkanji kofun | native_name =小見真観寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Omishinkanji Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.159417|N|139.476833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Q11463652|WD=Q11463652}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[:en:Schist|Schist]] quarried in the [[:en:Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Bushū-Araki Station|Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high

These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Bushū-Araki Station|Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high

The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[:en:Schist|Schist]] quarried in the [[:en:Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Bushū-Araki Station|Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high

==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[:en:Schist|Schist]] quarried in the [[:en:Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Bushū-Araki Station|Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[:en:Schist|Schist]] quarried in the [[:en:Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Bushū-Araki Station|Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Bushū-Araki Station|Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high

The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temple]] on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of [[:en:Schist|Schist]] quarried in the [[:en:Chichibu, Saitama|Chichibu region]]. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Bushū-Araki Station|Bushū-Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Chichibu Railway|Chichibu Railway]] [[:en:Chichibu Main Line|Chichibu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omi Shinkanji Kofun]][[en:Omishinkanji Kofun]][[ja:小見真観寺古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omi Shinkanji Kofun]][[en:Omishinkanji Kofun]][[ja:小見真観寺古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omi Shinkanji Kofun]][[en:Omishinkanji Kofun]][[ja:小見真観寺古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11463662]] Wikidata name: Omishinkanji Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Omishinkanji kofun | native_name =小見真観寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Omishinkanji Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.159417|N|139.476833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Q11463652|WD=Q11463652}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temple}} on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of {{ill|Schist|en|Schist|WD=Q15315|lt=Schist}} quarried in the {{ill|Chichibu, Saitama|en|Chichibu, Saitama|WD=Q647079|lt=Chichibu region}}. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Bushū-Araki Station|en|Bushū-Araki Station|WD=Q5001622|lt=Bushū-Araki Station}} on the {{ill|Chichibu Railway|en|Chichibu Railway|WD=Q843780|lt=Chichibu Railway}} {{ill|Chichibu Main Line|en|Chichibu Main Line|WD=Q5096033|lt=Chichibu Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|WD=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/omisinkanjikofun.html Gyoda City Board of Education] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temple}} on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of {{ill|Schist|en|Schist|WD=Q15315|lt=Schist}} quarried in the {{ill|Chichibu, Saitama|en|Chichibu, Saitama|WD=Q647079|lt=Chichibu region}}. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Bushū-Araki Station|en|Bushū-Araki Station|WD=Q5001622|lt=Bushū-Araki Station}} on the {{ill|Chichibu Railway|en|Chichibu Railway|WD=Q843780|lt=Chichibu Railway}} {{ill|Chichibu Main Line|en|Chichibu Main Line|WD=Q5096033|lt=Chichibu Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|WD=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/omisinkanjikofun.html Gyoda City Board of Education] {{in lang|ja}}

The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Bushū-Araki Station|en|Bushū-Araki Station|WD=Q5001622|lt=Bushū-Araki Station|1=Bushū-Araki Station}} on the {{ill|Chichibu Railway|en|Chichibu Railway|WD=Q843780|lt=Chichibu Railway|1=Chichibu Railway}} {{ill|Chichibu Main Line|en|Chichibu Main Line|WD=Q5096033|lt=Chichibu Main Line|1=Chichibu Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Omishinkanji kofun | native_name =小見真観寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Omishinkanji Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Omishinkanji kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.159417|N|139.476833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Q11463652|WD=Q11463652|1=Q11463652}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Omishinkanji Kofun'''|小見真観寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Omi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/162341|title=小見真観寺古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Omishinkanji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. it is the fourth largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Saitama prefecture. The tumulus is located within the precincts of Shinkan-ji, a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temple|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} on the right bank of the Hoshikawa, which flows east through the northern part of Gyōda. The tumulus has a total length of 102 meters and is orientated west-northwest. The main [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the south side of the posterior circular portion and was discovered in 1634. It is an unusually elaborate chamber in that it contained two rooms separated by a monolithic stone with square windows. The front chamber had a length of 2.7 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 m, and the entrance chamber had a length of 2.4 meters, width 2.2 meters and height 2.1 meters. The burial chamber was constructed of megaliths of {{ill|Schist|en|Schist|WD=Q15315|lt=Schist|1=Schist}} quarried in the {{ill|Chichibu, Saitama|en|Chichibu, Saitama|WD=Q647079|lt=Chichibu region|1=Chichibu, Saitama}}. A second burial chamber for a subsequent burial was located in the saddle area of the tumulus in 1880, and a large number of [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s, including gold rings, iron swords, bronze ornaments, and earthenware were discovered. These items are now stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. Later [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the tumulus had once been surrounded by a moat, from which many fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were recovered. These ''haniwa'' and the shape of the mound dates this tumulus to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is one of four tumuli forming the Omi Kofun Group. The {{nihongo|'''Kokuzoyama Kofun'''|虚空蔵山古墳||}} is another keyhole-shaped tumulus with length of 40 to 50 meters and is a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site. The other two tumuli in the cluster are smaller {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular-type mounds. The Omishinkanji Kofun is located about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Bushū-Araki Station|en|Bushū-Araki Station|WD=Q5001622|lt=Bushū-Araki Station|1=Bushū-Araki Station}} on the {{ill|Chichibu Railway|en|Chichibu Railway|WD=Q843780|lt=Chichibu Railway|1=Chichibu Railway}} {{ill|Chichibu Main Line|en|Chichibu Main Line|WD=Q5096033|lt=Chichibu Main Line|1=Chichibu Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 55 meter diameter x 7.8 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 48 meters wide x 7 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|WD=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
